title: Shinkansen accident in Hamamatsu causes service suspension and subsequent resumption
summary: One person dies after being hit by a bullet train at Hamamatsu Station, forcing all lines to stop for three hours and causing massive delays.

title: Tensions between US and Iran delay negotiations and affect navigation in the Persian Gulf
summary: Nuclear talks are postponed as Japanese ships evacuate the Persian Gulf amid rising tensions.

title: Fire in Tokyo elementary school causes eleven injured and mass evacuation
summary: A fire at a school in Kita district leaves eleven injured, three with fractures, and forces the evacuation of students.

title: Yen falls to four-decade lows and Government warns of intervention
summary: The currency weakens to 161 yen per dollar, while the Finance Minister threatens to take "drastic measures" against speculation.

title: Japanese government presents draft to reform imperial succession
summary: The plan allows the adoption of males from former aristocratic families to ensure dynastic stability.

title: Japanese national team faces World Cup with absences and controversy
summary: Japan prepares for key match against Tunisia without Kubo, while a former Dutch player apologizes for disparaging the team.

title: Japan registers multiple incidents in the educational field
summary: Cases of bullying, dangerous objects in backpacks, and changes to common exams mark the educational news.

title: Prosecution renounces proving guilt in Hino case and paves way for acquittal
summary: In the retrial, the prosecution will not present evidence of guilt, which will likely lead to acquittal.

title: Japan quintuples visa fee for foreigners
summary: From July, the fee will go from 3,000 to 15,000 yen.

title: Trump says it is time to deal with North Korea
summary: The former president talked with the South Korean president about the need to address the North Korean issue.

title: Mexico defeats South Korea and qualifies for World Cup round of 16
summary: With a solid defense, Mexico becomes the first team to advance to the next round.

title: Japanese emperors visit a children's cancer center in the Netherlands
summary: The emperors, together with the Dutch king and queen, toured the facilities of the pediatric hospital.

title: Kyoto Prize recognizes inventor of perovskite solar cells
summary: Tsutomu Miyabe is awarded for his contribution to solar energy, along with other laureates.

title: Disagreement persists over extension of Hokuriku Shinkansen
summary: The parties fail to reach consensus on the Obama-Kyoto section, while Kansai business leaders demand progress.

title: Japanet launches tender offer for Twinbird
summary: The catalog sales company seeks to acquire all shares of the home appliance manufacturer.

title: World Cup generates curious phenomena among Japanese fans
summary: Water consumption in Tokyo spikes during matches and criticism arises of Japanese fans abroad.

title: Japan designates new historical sites and natural monuments
summary: The Agency for Cultural Affairs declares several places, including castle ruins and waterfalls, as protected properties.

title: Japanese stock market reaches new records while Growth index falls
summary: The Nikkei 225 surpasses 71,000 points, while the Growth 250 hits five-month lows.

title: Several criminal cases shock Japan
summary: From a father who kills his son to a murder in a nursing home, the judiciary processes the defendants.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi faces internal and external challenges
summary: The leader meets with her party leaders to discuss taxes while facing criticism for her isolation at the G7 summit.
